steps.title: Use the roots
steps.content:
The factored form shows the two zeros of the quadratic. Set each factor equal to zero:
$$(x-14)(x+19)=0$$
So the zeros are $x=14$ and $x=-19$.

steps.title: Find the midpoint
steps.content:
For an upward-opening parabola, the minimum occurs at the vertex. The vertex is halfway between the two zeros:
$$\frac{14+(-19)}{2}=\frac{-5}{2}$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the function and read the $x$-coordinate of the vertex. The answer is D.
